e battle. =E2=80=9CThe streaming paradigm has been established,=E2=80=9D Fi=
shman wrote, declaring Netflix the champion, followed by Amazon and Disney=
=E2=80=A6 and everyone else well behind. =E2=80=9CThe case for consolidatio=
n among the subscale platforms is clear, and such an outcome increasingly s=

unt+ as the <em>most</em>

th Max,=E2=80=9D he admitted.<br/><br/>The silver lining for sports rights =

/li><li style=3D"Margin:0 0 24px 0;"><em>NASCAR=E2=80=99s new deal</em>: Wi=
th a bunch of crashes in the final laps, an unlikely back-to-back winner in=
 <strong>William Byron</strong>, and a highly competitive race throughout, =
yesterday=E2=80=99s chaotic Daytona 500 was a TV executive=E2=80=99s dream.=
 It also marked the official start of the racing circuit=E2=80=99s new seve=
n-year, $7.7 billion media rights deals, which will see races spread across=
 five media companies: Fox, NBC, Warner Bros. Discovery, Amazon Prime, and
Nexstar=E2=80=99s The CW. NASCAR discovered that the best way to ensure a r=
ights fee bump was to increase the number of media companies carrying races=
 from two to five. In strict financial terms, the move paid off, leading to=
 a 40 percent increase over the old rights agreements, to $1.1 billion per =
year.<br/><br/>Consumers have complained about the difficulty inherent in f=
inding the sports they want to watch in our multiplatform world. But NASCAR=
 president <strong>Steve Phelps </strong>has expressed
confidence that his league=E2=80=99s fans will find the races no matter whe=
re they are. As evidence, he has pointed to the 15 percent ratings jumps fo=
r the playoff races last season when they moved from USA Network to The CW.=
 And if you can find The CW these days, you can find anything=E2=80=A6</li>=
<li style=3D"Margin:0 0 24px 0;"><em>Grant Hill=E2=80=99s ownership path</e=
m>: Two years after Grant Hill retired from the NBA, he bought an ownership=
 stake in the Atlanta Hawks. He=E2=80=99s since grown his portfolio to incl=
ude
stakes in the Baltimore Orioles, Orlando City SC, and Orlando Pride. On thi=
s week=E2=80=99s <em>Varsity </em>podcast, I asked about his path to owners=
hip. Hill referenced his father, NFL great <strong>Calvin</strong> <strong>=
Hill</strong>, a Super Bowl champion running back and successful sports bus=
iness executive who tried unsuccessfully to buy stakes in various teams ove=

r during the Super Bowl. (All those ad sales certainly contributed to the <=

"margin:0;
margin-bottom:0px;">The weekend=E2=80=99s business success goes way beyond =
the host broadcaster. San Francisco was crawling with executives from nearl=
y every mediaco, corporate sponsor, and the biggest of Big Tech companies. =
Sports=E2=80=99 biggest brands, including Jordan and DraftKings, hosted par=
ties, the latter of which featured a performance by Shaq=E2=80=99s alter eg=
o, =E2=80=9CDJ Diesel.=E2=80=9D But it wasn=E2=80=99t all superficial, eith=
er. ASW has become second only to the Super Bowl as a convening hub of leag=
ue professionals, media
partners, and <em>Fortune </em>500 C.M.O.s. The NBA doesn=E2=80=99t attract=
 this kind of attention during the NBA Finals or, really, at any other time=
=2E Similarly, MLB and the NHL position=
 their midseason breaks to foster thes=
e sorts of marketing opportunities, as well.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;=
margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">For =
the past 25 years, of course, the NBA has hosted a Technology Summit on the=
 Friday before the game. Billed as an off-the-record event,
the league uses it as a platform for current and future partners=E2=80=94<s=
trong>Jimmy Pitaro</strong>, <strong>Eddy Cue</strong>, <strong>Bela Bajari=
a</strong>, and <strong>Neal Mohan </strong>were in attendance=E2=80=94to s=
peak under the soft lights. But while the conference is happening, the lobb=
y just outside the summit is one of the best networking events in the sport=
s business.<br/><br/></p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">So yes, t=
he NBA has an All-Star Game problem, but it=E2=80=99s <em>not </em>a busine=
ss
problem. Indeed, the NBA=E2=80=99s ratings have rebounded from those early-=
season doldrums, and the league is openly discussing making some rules chan=
ges to address criticisms about the flow of the game. As <strong>Grant Hill=
 </strong>wisely <a href=3D"https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImR=
nVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dWRnRITlc3ckxpaVJfY2ZneS00WT0iLCJocmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly=

b16e415f1f57994852696115e3001229c4d2b9b" style=3D"text-decoration:underline=
;color:#d40000;">noted</a> on my podcast, <em>The Varsity</em>, the league =
has never shied away from changing the rules to improve the game=E2=80=94fr=
om adding the
three-point line, to the advent of zone defense, getting rid of hand-checki=
ng in the mid-aughts, and so forth. After all, basketball is played the way=
 it is today <em>because </em>of rules changes made a generation ago.<br/><=
br/></p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">Along those lines, the NBA=
 does have some other ideas for tweaking the All-Star Game format, includin=
g a U.S. versus the world showdown. The NHL has received praise for its cur=
rent 4 Nations Face-Off tournament, based in part on
the belief that the teams are actually trying to win it! (Also, ABC=E2=80=
=99s coverage of the U.S.-Canada game on Saturday night drew 4.4 million vi=
ewers=E2=80=94the biggest non-Stanley Cup Final audience in five years.) Fo=
reign-born NBA superstars like <strong>Victor Wembanyama</strong> (France) =
and <strong>Giannis Antetokounmpo</strong> (Greece) have voiced their suppo=
rt for the idea, and the league certainly has the personnel to make it work=

(<strong>SGA</strong>), Serbia (<strong>Don=C4=8Di=C4=87</strong>), and, we=
ll=E2=80=A6 New Jersey (<strong>KAT</strong>).</p><p style=3D"margin:0;marg=
in-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;">The NBA is also considerin=
g bringing back the format that pitted the Eastern Conference All-Stars aga=
inst their Western Conference counterparts. Even though that arrangement ev=
entually devolved into defense-free, 200-198=E2=80=93style contests that la=
mpooned the actual game, well, it may just seem retro enough to be cool the=
se
days.</p></div>
